question: Find the name of principal investigators in projects that started in 2014
ground_truth: SELECT T2.full_name FROM projects AS T1 join people as T2 on T1.principal_investigator = T2.unics_id WHERE T1.start_year = 2014
generated_sql:
SELECT p.principal_investigator, p.title
FROM projects p
JOIN people pi ON p.principal_investigator = pi.unics_id
WHERE p.start_year = 2014;

question: Show the full name of people being investigators in projects that began in 2014
ground_truth: SELECT T2.full_name FROM projects AS T1 join people as T2 on T1.principal_investigator = T2.unics_id WHERE T1.start_year = 2014
generated_sql:
SELECT people.full_name
FROM people
JOIN project_members ON people.unics_id = project_members.member_role
JOIN projects ON project_members.project = projects.unics_id
WHERE project_members.member_role = 'Investigator'
AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM projects.start_date) = 2014;
exec_status: SQL Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedFunction) operator does not exist: integer = text

question: Find projects whose subject area is “Mathematics and Statistics”
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title FROM projects AS T1 join project_subject_areas as psa on T1.unics_id = psa.project join subject_areas as sa on psa.subject_area = sa.code WHERE sa.title = 'Mathematics and Statistics'
generated_sql: SELECT p.unics_id, p.title, p.ec_ref, p.start_date, p.end_date FROM projects p JOIN subject_areas sa ON p.ec_fund_scheme = 'Mathematics and Statistics';

question: Show the acronym and subject area description of projects with subject area “Mathematics and Statistics”
ground_truth: SELECT t1.acronym, sa.description FROM projects AS T1 join project_subject_areas as psa on T1.unics_id = psa.project join subject_areas as sa on psa.subject_area = sa.code WHERE sa.title = 'Mathematics and Statistics'
generated_sql:
SELECT p.acronym, s.title
FROM projects p
JOIN project_subject_areas ps ON p.unics_id = ps.project
JOIN subject_areas s ON ps.subject_area = s.code
WHERE s.title = 'Mathematics and Statistics';

question: Find all different topics of projects that ended in 2014
ground_truth: SELECT distinct t.title FROM projects AS T1 join project_topics as pt on T1.unics_id = pt.project join topics as t on pt.topic = t.code where t1.end_year = 2014
generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT t.topic
FROM topics t
JOIN project_topics pt ON t.code = pt.topic
JOIN projects p ON pt.project = p.unics_id
WHERE p.end_date BETWE0 2014-01-01 AND p.end_date < 2015-01-01;
exec_status: SQL Error: (psycopg2.errors.SyntaxError) syntax error at or near "BETWE0"

question: Find distinct titles of topics of projects that with end year 2014

generated_sql: SELECT DISTINCT title FROM topics WHERE code IN (SELECT topic FROM project_topics WHERE project IN (SELECT unics_id FROM projects WHERE end_year = 2014));

question: Show the acronym of projects with a member from Greece
ground_truth: Select p.acronym from projects as p join project_members as pm on p.unics_id = pm.project join institutions as i on pm.institution_id = i.unics_id join countries as c on i.country_id = c.unics_id where c.country_name = 'Greece'
generated_sql:
SELECT p.acronym
FROM projects p
JOIN project_members pm ON p.unics_id = pm.project
JOIN institutions i ON pm.institution_id = i.unics_id
WHERE i.country_id = (SELECT unics_id FROM countries WHERE country_name = 'Greece');

question: Show the title of the project with the highest total cost

question: Show the name of members in projects costing less than the average project cost.
ground_truth: SELECT T1.member_name FROM project_members AS T1 JOIN projects AS T2 ON T1.project = T2.unics_id WHERE T2.total_cost < (SELECT AVG(T23.total_cost) FROM projects AS T23)
generated_sql:
SELECT p.title AS project_name, m.member_name
FROM projects p
JOIN project_members m ON p.unics_id = m.project
WHERE p.total_cost < (SELECT AVG(total_cost) FROM projects);

question: Find the id of projects with at least 100 members
ground_truth: select p.unics_id from projects as p join project_members as pm on p.unics_id = pm.project group by p.unics_id having count(p.unics_id) >= 100
generated_sql: SELECT p.unics_id FROM projects p JOIN project_members pm ON p.unics_id = pm.project WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM project_members WHERE project = p.unics_id) >= 100;

question: Find the code and title of the funding scheme which funded the highest number of projects.

generated_sql: SELECT f.code, f.title FROM funding_schemes f JOIN project_members pm ON f.code = pm.ec_fund_scheme WHERE pm.ec_fund_scheme IS NOT NULL GROUP BY f.code, f.title ORDER BY COUNT(pm.project) DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Find the projects which lasted the longest and have an end year and a start year

question: Which institutions have never been project coordinators
ground_truth: SELECT distinct member_name from project_members WHERE member_name NOT in (SELECT member_name from project_members where member_role = 'coordinator')
generated_sql:
WITH CoordinatorInstitutions AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT institutions.unics_id
  FROM project_members
  JOIN project_erc_panels ON project_members.project = project_erc_panels.project
  JOIN erc_panels ON project_erc_panels.panel = erc_panels.code
  JOIN institutions ON project_members.institution_id = institutions.unics_id
)
SELECT DISTINCT institutions.unics_id, institutions.institutions_name
FROM institutions
WHERE institutions.unics_id NOT IN (SELECT unics_id FROM CoordinatorInstitutions);

question: Show all of the instituions that have never coordinated a project
ground_truth: SELECT distinct member_name from project_members WHERE member_name NOT in (SELECT member_name from project_members where member_role = 'coordinator')
generated_sql:
WITH coordinated_institutions AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT institutions.unics_id
  FROM project_members
  JOIN project_erc_panels ON project_members.project = project_erc_panels.project
  JOIN erc_panels ON project_erc_panels.panel = erc_panels.code
  JOIN institutions ON project_members.institution_id = institutions.unics_id
)
SELECT DISTINCT institutions.unics_id, institutions.institutions_name
FROM institutions
WHERE institutions.unics_id NOT IN (SELECT unics_id FROM coordinated_institutions);

question: Show all of the names of people who have worked on multiple projects
ground_truth: SELECT t1.full_name, count(p.title) FROM people as t1 join projects as p on t1.unics_id = p.principal_investigator group by t1.full_name HAVING count(p.title) > 1
generated_sql:
SELECT p.principal_investigator, COUNT(pm.project) AS num_projects
FROM projects p
JOIN project_members pm ON p.unics_id = pm.project
GROUP BY p.principal_investigator
HAVING COUNT(pm.project) > 1;

question: Which people have worked on more than one project
ground_truth: SELECT t1.full_name, count(p.title) FROM people as t1 join projects as p on t1.unics_id = p.principal_investigator group by t1.full_name HAVING count(p.title) > 1
generated_sql:
SELECT p.principal_investigator, COUNT(pm.project) AS project_count
FROM projects p
JOIN project_members pm ON p.unics_id = pm.project
GROUP BY p.principal_investigator
HAVING COUNT(pm.project) > 1;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding
FROM subject_areas
JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area
JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id
GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the highest funding contribution?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(project_members.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
FROM project_members
JOIN project_subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.project = project_members.project
JOIN subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area
GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the least funding contribution
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MIN(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MIN(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(project_members.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
FROM project_members
JOIN project_subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.project = project_members.project
JOIN subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area
GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding ASC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the least amount of funding

question: Which funding scheme provided the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, sum(ec_max_contribution) from funding_schemes as t1 join projects as t2 on t1.code = t2.ec_fund_scheme group by t1.title ORDER BY sum(ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT f.title, SUM(p.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
FROM projects p
JOIN project_programmes pp ON p.unics_id = pp.project
JOIN programmes pr ON pp.programme = pr.code
JOIN funding_schemes f ON pr.code = f.code
GROUP BY f.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;
exec_status: SQL Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column p.ec_contribution does not exist

question: Show the amount of funding and corresponding list of industrial partners that have worked on projects in order from the most funding to the least funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t2.code = 'PRC' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC
generated_sql:
SELECT p.total_cost, i.institution_name, p.ec_max_contribution
FROM projects p
JOIN project_members pm ON p.unics_id = pm.project

question: Which non-EU entity has recieved the most funding through a project?

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql: SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding FROM subject_areas JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id GROUP BY subject_areas.title ORDER BY total_funding DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the highest funding contribution?

generated_sql: SELECT subject_areas.description FROM subject_areas JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id ORDER BY projects.ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the least funding contribution

generated_sql: SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(projects.ec_max_contribution) as total_contribution FROM subject_areas JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id GROUP BY subject_areas.title ORDER BY total_contribution ASC LIMIT 1;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the least amount of funding

question: Find the code and title of the funding scheme which funded the highest number of projects.
ground_truth: select s.code, s.title from funding_schemes as s where s.code = (select s2.code from funding_schemes as s2 join projects as p on s2.code = p.ec_fund_scheme group by s2.code order by count(*) desc LIMIT 1)

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding

exec_status: SQL Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column countries.geocode_regions_3 does not exist

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql: SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding FROM subject_areas JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id GROUP BY subject_areas.title ORDER BY total_funding DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the highest funding contribution?

generated_sql: SELECT subject_areas.description FROM subject_areas JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id ORDER BY projects.ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the least funding contribution
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MIN(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MIN(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql: SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(projects.ec_max_contribution) as total_contribution FROM subject_areas JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id GROUP BY subject_areas.title ORDER BY total_contribution ASC LIMIT 1;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the least amount of funding

question: Find the name of principal investigators in projects that started in 2014
ground_truth: SELECT T2.full_name FROM projects AS T1 join people as T2 on T1.principal_investigator = T2.unics_id WHERE T1.start_year = 2014
generated_sql:
SELECT p.principal_investigator
FROM projects p
JOIN people pi ON p.principal_investigator = pi.unics_id
WHERE p.start_year = 2014;

question: Show the full name of people being investigators in projects that began in 2014
ground_truth: SELECT T2.full_name FROM projects AS T1 join people as T2 on T1.principal_investigator = T2.unics_id WHERE T1.start_year = 2014
generated_sql:
WITH investigators AS (
  SELECT p.unics_id, p.principal_investigator
  FROM projects p
  WHERE p.start_year = 2014
)
SELECT i.principal_investigator, p.full_name
FROM investigators i
JOIN people p ON i.principal_investigator = p.unics_id;

question: Find projects whose subject area is “Mathematics and Statistics”
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title FROM projects AS T1 join project_subject_areas as psa on T1.unics_id = psa.project join subject_areas as sa on psa.subject_area = sa.code WHERE sa.title = 'Mathematics and Statistics'
generated_sql:
SELECT p.unics_id, p.title, p.ec_ref, p.start_date, p.end_date
FROM projects p
JOIN subject_areas sa ON p.ec_fund_scheme = sa.code
WHERE sa.title = 'Mathematics and Statistics';

question: Show the acronym and subject area description of projects with subject area “Mathematics and Statistics”
ground_truth: SELECT t1.acronym, sa.description FROM projects AS T1 join project_subject_areas as psa on T1.unics_id = psa.project join subject_areas as sa on psa.subject_area = sa.code WHERE sa.title = 'Mathematics and Statistics'
generated_sql:
SELECT p.acronym, s.title
FROM projects p
JOIN project_subject_areas ps ON p.unics_id = ps.project
JOIN subject_areas s ON ps.subject_area = s.code

question: Find the code and title of the funding scheme which funded the highest number of projects.
ground_truth: select s.code, s.title from funding_schemes as s where s.code = (select s2.code from funding_schemes as s2 join projects as p on s2.code = p.ec_fund_scheme group by s2.code order by count(*) desc LIMIT 1)
generated_sql:
WITH funded_projects AS (
  SELECT
    f.code,
    COUNT(p.unics_id) AS project_count
  FROM
    funding_schemes f
    JOIN project_members pm ON f.code = pm.funding_scheme
  JOIN projects p ON pm.project = p.unics_id
  GROUP BY
    f.code
),
max_funded_projects AS (
  SELECT
    code,
    project_count
  FROM
    funded_projects
  ORDER BY
    project_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  f.code,
  f.title
FROM
  funding_schemes f
JOIN
  max_funded_projects mfp ON f.code = mfp.code;
exec_status: Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column pm.funding_scheme does not exist

question: Find the projects which lasted the longest and have an end year and a start year
ground_truth: select title, end_year - start_year from projects order by end_year - start_year desc limit 1

WITH LongestProjects AS (
  SELECT
    projects.unics_id,
    projects.title,
    projects.start_year,
    projects.end_year,
    projects.end_year - projects.start_year AS duration
  FROM
    projects
  WHERE
    projects.start_year IS NOT NULL
    AND projects.end_year IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  unics_id,
  title,
  start_year,
  end_year,
  duration
FROM
  LongestProjects
ORDER BY
  duration DESC
LIMIT 10;

GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which non-EU entity has recieved the most funding through a project?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t1.geocode_regions_3 = 'NONEU' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
WITH EU_Funding AS (
  SELECT
    p.title AS project_title,
    SUM(p.total_cost) AS total_eu_funding
  FROM
    projects p
  JOIN
    project_programmes pp ON p.unics_id = pp.project
  JOIN
    programmes pr ON pp.programme = pr.code
  JOIN
    ec_framework_programs efp ON pr.ec_framework_program = efp.ec_framework_program_name
  WHERE
    efp.ec_framework_program_name IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY
    p.title
  ORDER BY
    total_eu_funding DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  p.title AS project_title,
  p.ec_ref AS project_ec_ref,
  p.total_cost AS total_;
exec_status: Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column pr.ec_framework_program does not exist

question: Which entity located outside of the EU has received the most funding?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t1.geocode_regions_3 = 'NONEU' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
WITH EU_Funding AS (
  SELECT
    p.title AS project_title,
    p.total_cost
  FROM
    projects p
  JOIN
    institutions i ON p.institution_id = i.unics_id
  WHERE
    i.geocode_regions_3 NOT LIKE 'EU%'
  ORDER BY
    p.total_cost DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  project_title,
  total_cost
FROM
  EU_Funding;
exec_status: Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column p.institution_id does not exist

question: Which non-EU entity has recieved the least funding through a project?

  SELECT
    p.title AS project_title,
    p.total_cost AS total_eu_funding,
    p.ec_max_contribution AS total_non_eu_funding
  FROM
    projects p
  JOIN

)

FROM

ORDER BY

LIMIT 1;

question: Find the code and title of the funding scheme which funded the highest number of projects.
ground_truth: select s.code, s.title from funding_schemes as s where s.code = (select s2.code from funding_schemes as s2 join projects as p on s2.code = p.ec_fund_scheme group by s2.code order by count(*) desc LIMIT 1)
generated_sql:
WITH funded_projects AS (
  SELECT
    f.code,
    COUNT(p.unics_id) AS project_count
  FROM
    funding_schemes f
    JOIN project_members pm ON f.code = pm.funding_scheme
  JOIN projects p ON pm.project = p.unics_id
  GROUP BY
    f.code
),
max_funded_projects AS (
  SELECT
    code,
    project_count
  FROM
    funded_projects
  ORDER BY
    project_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  f.code,
  f.title
FROM
  funding_schemes f
JOIN
  max_funded_projects mfp ON f.code = mfp.code;
exec_status: Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column pm.funding_scheme does not exist

question: Find the projects which lasted the longest and have an end year and a start year
ground_truth: select title, end_year - start_year from projects order by end_year - start_year desc limit 1
generated_sql:
WITH LongestProjects AS (
  SELECT
    projects.unics_id,
    projects.title,
    projects.start_year,
    projects.end_year,
    projects.end_year - projects.start_year AS duration
  FROM
    projects
  WHERE
    projects.start_year IS NOT NULL
    AND projects.end_year IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  unics_id,
  title,
  start_year,
  end_year,
  duration
FROM
  LongestProjects
ORDER BY
  duration DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: Which institutions have never been project coordinators

  SELECT DISTINCT project_members.project
  FROM project_members
  JOIN project_members ON project_members.project = project_members.project
)

FROM institutions
WHERE institutions.unics_id NOT IN (SELECT project_members.project FROM coordinators);

question: Show all of the names of people who have worked on multiple projects
ground_truth: SELECT t1.full_name, count(p.title) FROM people as t1 join projects as p on t1.unics_id = p.principal_investigator group by t1.full_name HAVING count(p.title) > 1
generated_sql:
WITH project_counts AS (
  SELECT
    p.principal_investigator,
    COUNT(p.unics_id) AS project_count
  FROM
    projects p
  GROUP BY
    p.principal_investigator
  HAVING
    COUNT(p.unics_id) > 1
)
SELECT
  p.principal_investigator,
  p.title
FROM
  projects p
JOIN
  project_counts pc ON p.principal_investigator = pc.principal_investigator;

question: Which people have worked on more than one project
ground_truth: SELECT t1.full_name, count(p.title) FROM people as t1 join projects as p on t1.unics_id = p.principal_investigator group by t1.full_name HAVING count(p.title) > 1
generated_sql:
WITH project_counts AS (
  SELECT
    p.principal_investigator,
    COUNT(p.unics_id) AS project_count
  FROM
    projects p
  GROUP BY
    p.principal_investigator
  HAVING
    COUNT(p.unics_id) > 1
)
SELECT
  p.principal_investigator,
  p.unics_id,
  p.title,
  p.start_date,
  p.end_date
FROM
  projects p
INNER JOIN
  project_counts pc
ON
  p.principal_investigator = pc.principal_investigator;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding
FROM subject_areas
JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area
JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id
GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the highest funding contribution?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
WITH subject_area_funding AS (
  SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(project_members.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
  FROM project_subject_areas
  JOIN project_members ON project_subject_areas.project = project_members.project
  JOIN subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.subject_area = subject_areas.code
  GROUP BY subject_areas.title
)
SELECT title, total_funding
FROM subject_area_funding
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the least funding contribution

generated_sql:
WITH subject_area_funding AS (
  SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(project_members.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
  FROM project_members
  JOIN project_subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.project = project_members.project
  JOIN subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area

)
SELECT title, total_funding
FROM subject_area_funding
ORDER BY total_funding ASC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the least amount of funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MIN(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MIN(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
WITH SubjectAreaFunding AS (
  SELECT
    subject_areas.title,
    SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding
  FROM
    subject_areas
  JOIN
    project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area
  JOIN
    projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id

)
SELECT

  total_funding
FROM

ORDER BY
  total_funding ASC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which funding scheme provided the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, sum(ec_max_contribution) from funding_schemes as t1 join projects as t2 on t1.code = t2.ec_fund_scheme group by t1.title ORDER BY sum(ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT funding_schemes.title, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding
FROM funding_schemes
JOIN projects ON funding_schemes.code = projects.ec_fund_scheme
GROUP BY funding_schemes.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC

question: Which funding scheme provided the least funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, sum(ec_max_contribution) from funding_schemes as t1 join projects as t2 on t1.code = t2.ec_fund_scheme group by t1.title ORDER BY sum(ec_max_contribution) ASC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT f.title AS "Funding Scheme", MIN(p.total_cost) AS "Least Funding"
FROM projects p

question: Show the industrial partners that have worked on projects and the amount of funding they have recieved in order from most to least
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t2.code = 'PRC' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC
generated_sql:
WITH funding_by_partner AS (
  SELECT
    project_members.institution_id,
    institutions.country_id,
    institutions.institutions_name,
    SUM(project_members.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
  FROM
    project_members
  JOIN
    institutions ON project_members.institution_id = institutions.unics_id
  GROUP BY
    project_members.institution_id,
    institutions.country_id,
    institutions.institutions_name
)

SELECT
  institutions.institutions_name,
  institutions.country_id,
  funding_by_partner.total_funding
FROM
  funding_by_partner
JOIN
  institutions ON funding_by_partner.institution_id = institutions.unics_id;

question: Which non-EU entity has recieved the most funding through a project?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t1.geocode_regions_3 = 'NONEU' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
WITH EU_Funding AS (
  SELECT
    p.title AS project_title,
    SUM(p.total_cost) AS total_eu_funding
  FROM
    projects p
  JOIN
    project_programmes pp ON p.unics_id = pp.project
  JOIN
    programmes pr ON pp.programme = pr.code
  JOIN
    ec_framework_programs efp ON pr.ec_framework_program = efp.ec_framework_program_name
  WHERE
    efp.ec_framework_program_name IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY
    p.title
  ORDER BY
    total_eu_funding DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  p.title AS project_title,
  p.ec_ref AS project_ec_ref,
  p.total_cost AS total_;
exec_status: Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column pr.ec_framework_program does not exist

question: Which entity located outside of the EU has received the most funding?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t1.geocode_regions_3 = 'NONEU' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
WITH EU_Funding AS (
  SELECT
    p.title AS project_title,
    p.total_cost
  FROM
    projects p
  JOIN
    institutions i ON p.institution_id = i.unics_id
  WHERE
    i.geocode_regions_3 NOT LIKE 'EU%'
  ORDER BY
    p.total_cost DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  project_title,
  total_cost

question: Find the code and title of the funding scheme which funded the highest number of projects.
ground_truth: select s.code, s.title from funding_schemes as s where s.code = (select s2.code from funding_schemes as s2 join projects as p on s2.code = p.ec_fund_scheme group by s2.code order by count(*) desc LIMIT 1)
generated_sql:
WITH funded_projects AS (
  SELECT
    f.code,
    COUNT(p.unics_id) AS project_count
  FROM
    funding_schemes f
    JOIN project_members pm ON f.code = pm.funding_scheme
  JOIN projects p ON pm.project = p.unics_id
  GROUP BY
    f.code
),
max_funded_projects AS (
  SELECT
    code,
    project_count
  FROM
    funded_projects
  ORDER BY
    project_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  f.code,
  f.title
FROM
  funding_schemes f
JOIN
  max_funded_projects mfp ON f.code = mfp.code;

question: Find the projects which lasted the longest and have an end year and a start year

WITH LongestProjects AS (
  SELECT
    projects.unics_id,
    projects.title,
    projects.start_year,
    projects.end_year,
    projects.end_year - projects.start_year AS duration
  FROM
    projects
  WHERE
    projects.start_year IS NOT NULL
    AND projects.end_year IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  unics_id,
  title,
  start_year,
  end_year,
  duration
FROM
  LongestProjects
ORDER BY
  duration DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: Find the code and title of the funding scheme which funded the highest number of projects.
ground_truth: select s.code, s.title from funding_schemes as s where s.code = (select s2.code from funding_schemes as s2 join projects as p on s2.code = p.ec_fund_scheme group by s2.code order by count(*) desc LIMIT 1)
generated_sql:
WITH funded_projects AS (
  SELECT
    f.code,
    COUNT(p.unics_id) AS project_count
  FROM
    funding_schemes f
    JOIN project_members pm ON f.code = pm.funding_scheme
  JOIN projects p ON pm.project = p.unics_id
  GROUP BY
    f.code
),
max_funded_projects AS (
  SELECT
    code,
    project_count
  FROM
    funded_projects
  ORDER BY
    project_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  f.code,
  f.title
FROM
  funding_schemes f
JOIN
  max_funded_projects mfp ON f.code = mfp.code;
exec_status: Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column pm.funding_scheme does not exist

question: Find the projects which lasted the longest and have an end year and a start year
ground_truth: select title, end_year - start_year from projects order by end_year - start_year desc limit 1
generated_sql:
WITH LongestProjects AS (
  SELECT
    projects.unics_id,
    projects.title,
    projects.start_year,
    projects.end_year,
    projects.end_year - projects.start_year AS duration
  FROM
    projects
  WHERE
    projects.start_year IS NOT NULL
    AND projects.end_year IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  unics_id,
  title,
  start_year,
  end_year,
  duration
FROM
  LongestProjects
ORDER BY
  duration DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding
FROM subject_areas
JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area
JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id
GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the highest funding contribution?

generated_sql:
WITH subject_area_funding AS (
  SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(project_members.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
  FROM project_subject_areas
  JOIN project_members ON project_subject_areas.project = project_members.project
  JOIN subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.subject_area = subject_areas.code
  GROUP BY subject_areas.title
)
SELECT title, total_funding
FROM subject_area_funding
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the least amount of funding

generated_sql:
WITH SubjectAreaFunding AS (
  SELECT
    subject_areas.title,
    SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding
  FROM
    subject_areas
  JOIN
    project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area

)
SELECT

  total_funding
FROM
  SubjectAreaFunding
ORDER BY
  total_funding ASC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which non-EU entity has recieved the most funding through a project?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t1.geocode_regions_3 = 'NONEU' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
WITH EU_Funding AS (
  SELECT
    p.title AS project_title,
    SUM(p.total_cost) AS total_eu_funding
  FROM
    projects p
  JOIN
    project_programmes pp ON p.unics_id = pp.project
  JOIN
    programmes pr ON pp.programme = pr.code
  JOIN
    ec_framework_programs efp ON pr.ec_framework_program = efp.ec_framework_program_name
  WHERE
    efp.ec_framework_program_name IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY
    p.title
  ORDER BY
    total_eu_funding DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
  p.title AS project_title,
  p.ec_ref AS project_ec_ref,
  p.total_cost AS total_;
exec_status: Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column pr.ec_framework_program does not exist

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding

JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id
GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which funding scheme provided the most funding

generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM erc_panels JOIN erc_research_domains ON erc_panels.part_of = erc_research_domains.code WHERE erc_research_domains.description LIKE '%Life Sciences%';

question: List all of the european research council panels related to social sciences and humanities
ground_truth: SELECT t1.description from erc_panels as t1 join erc_research_domains as t2 on t2.code = t1.part_of WHERE t2.code = 'SH'
generated_sql: SELECT erc_panels.description FROM erc_panels JOIN erc_research_domains ON erc_panels.part_of = erc_research_domains.code WHERE erc_research_domains.description LIKE '%social sciences%' OR erc_research_domains.description LIKE '%humanities%';

question: How many european research council panels are related to social sciences and humanities
ground_truth: SELECT count(t1.description) from erc_panels as t1 join erc_research_domains as t2 on t2.code = t1.part_of WHERE t2.code = 'SH'
generated_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM erc_research_domains INNER JOIN erc_panels ON erc_research_domains.code = erc_panels.part_of WHERE erc_research_domains.description LIKE '%social sciences and humanities%';

question: Show all of the project members that are private for profit entities
ground_truth: SELECT member_name FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code WHERE t2.code = 'PRC'
generated_sql: SELECT project_members.member_name, project_members.member_role, project_members.country FROM project_members JOIN institutions ON project_members.institution_id = institutions.unics_id WHERE project_members.activity_type = 'Private for profit' AND project_members.country = 'United Kingdom';

question: Which entity located outside of the EU has received the most funding?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t1.geocode_regions_3 = 'NONEU' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql: SELECT countries.country_name, SUM(projects.total_cost) as total_funding FROM countries JOIN projects ON countries.unics_id = projects.principal_investigator WHERE countries.geocode_country_code NOT LIKE 'EU%' GROUP BY countries.country_name ORDER BY total_funding DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which non-EU entity has recieved the most funding through a project?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t1.geocode_regions_3 = 'NONEU' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql: SELECT countries.country_name, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding FROM countries JOIN project_members ON countries.unics_id = project_members.country TEXT, projects WHERE project_members.project = projects.unics_id GROUP BY countries.country_name ORDER BY total_funding DESC LIMIT 1;
exec_status: SQL Error: (psycopg2.errors.SyntaxError) syntax error at or near "TEXT"

question: What is the number of projects that have been carried out in the subject area of Robotics?
ground_truth: SELECT count(t3.title) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id WHERE t1.title = 'Robotics'
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM project_subject_areas JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id JOIN subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.subject_area = subject_areas.code WHERE subject_areas.title = 'Robotics';

question: How many projects have been related to Robotics?
ground_truth: SELECT count(t3.title) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id WHERE t1.title = 'Robotics'
generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM project_subject_areas WHERE subject_area = 'Robotics';

question: Show institutions from France except the ones from Paris
ground_truth: select i.institutions_name from institutions as i join countries as c on c.unics_id = i.country_id where country_name = 'France' except select i.institutions_name from institutions as i join countries as c on c.unics_id = i.country_id join eu_territorial_units as etu on i.geocode_regions_3 = etu.geocode_regions where country_name = 'France' and etu.description = 'Paris'
generated_sql: SELECT institutions.institutions_name FROM institutions JOIN countries ON institutions.country_id = countries.unics_id WHERE countries.country_name = 'France' AND countries.country_code2 != 'FR';

question: Which institutions have never been project coordinators

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql: SELECT subject_areas.title FROM subject_areas JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id GROUP BY subject_areas.title ORDER BY SUM(projects.total_cost) DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the highest funding contribution?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql: SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(projects.ec_max_contribution) as total_contribution FROM project_subject_areas JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id JOIN subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.subject_area = subject_areas.code GROUP BY subject_areas.title ORDER BY total_contribution DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the least funding contribution
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MIN(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MIN(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql: SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(project_members.ec_contribution) as total_contribution FROM project_members JOIN project_subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.project = project_members.project JOIN subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.subject_area = subject_areas.code GROUP BY subject_areas.title ORDER BY total_contribution ASC LIMIT 1;

question: Which non-EU entity has recieved the least funding through a project?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t1.geocode_regions_3 = 'NONEU' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution ASC LIMIT 1

question: Which entity located outside of the EU has received the least funding?

generated_sql: SELECT countries.country_name, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding FROM countries JOIN institutions ON countries.unics_id = institutions.country_id JOIN project_members ON institutions.unics_id = project_members.institution_id JOIN projects ON project_members.project = projects.unics_id WHERE countries.geocode_country_code != 'EU' GROUP BY countries.country_name ORDER BY total_funding ASC LIMIT 1;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding

question: Which non-EU entity has recieved the most funding through a project?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t1.geocode_regions_3 = 'NONEU' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT countries.country_name, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding
FROM countries
JOIN institutions ON countries.unics_id = institutions.country_id

GROUP BY countries.country_name
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which institutions have never been project coordinators
ground_truth: SELECT distinct member_name from project_members WHERE member_name NOT in (SELECT member_name from project_members where member_role = 'coordinator')
generated_sql:
WITH CoordinatorInstitutions AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT institutions.unics_id
  FROM project_members
  JOIN project_erc_panels ON project_members.project = project_erc_panels.project
  JOIN erc_panels ON project_erc_panels.panel = erc_panels.code
  JOIN institutions ON project_members.institution_id = institutions.unics_id
)
SELECT DISTINCT institutions.unics_id, institutions.institutions_name
FROM institutions
WHERE institutions.unics_id NOT IN (SELECT unics_id FROM CoordinatorInstitutions);

question: Show all of the instituions that have never coordinated a project
ground_truth: SELECT distinct member_name from project_members WHERE member_name NOT in (SELECT member_name from project_members where member_role = 'coordinator')
generated_sql:
WITH coordinated_institutions AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT institutions.unics_id
  FROM project_members
  JOIN project_erc_panels ON project_members.project = project_erc_panels.project
  JOIN erc_panels ON project_erc_panels.panel = erc_panels.code
  JOIN institutions ON project_members.institution_id = institutions.unics_id
)
SELECT DISTINCT institutions.unics_id, institutions.institutions_name
FROM institutions
WHERE institutions.unics_id NOT IN (SELECT unics_id FROM coordinated_institutions);

question: Show all of the names of people who have worked on multiple projects
ground_truth: SELECT t1.full_name, count(p.title) FROM people as t1 join projects as p on t1.unics_id = p.principal_investigator group by t1.full_name HAVING count(p.title) > 1
generated_sql:
SELECT p.principal_investigator, COUNT(pm.project) AS num_projects
FROM projects p
JOIN project_members pm ON p.unics_id = pm.project
GROUP BY p.principal_investigator
HAVING COUNT(pm.project) > 1;

question: Which people have worked on more than one project
ground_truth: SELECT t1.full_name, count(p.title) FROM people as t1 join projects as p on t1.unics_id = p.principal_investigator group by t1.full_name HAVING count(p.title) > 1
generated_sql:
SELECT p.principal_investigator, COUNT(pm.project) AS project_count
FROM projects p
JOIN project_members pm ON p.unics_id = pm.project
GROUP BY p.principal_investigator
HAVING COUNT(pm.project) > 1;

question: What is the title of the subject area that has been granted the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(projects.total_cost) AS total_funding
FROM subject_areas
JOIN project_subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area
JOIN projects ON project_subject_areas.project = projects.unics_id
GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the highest funding contribution?
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) from subject_areas as t1 join project_subject_areas as t2 on t1.code = t2.subject_area join projects as t3 on t2.project = t3.unics_id  group by t1.title order by  MAX(t3.ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(project_members.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
FROM project_members
JOIN project_subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.project = project_members.project
JOIN subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area
GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which subject area has recieved the least funding contribution

generated_sql:
SELECT subject_areas.title, SUM(project_members.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
FROM project_members
JOIN project_subject_areas ON project_subject_areas.project = project_members.project
JOIN subject_areas ON subject_areas.code = project_subject_areas.subject_area
GROUP BY subject_areas.title
ORDER BY total_funding ASC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which funding scheme provided the most funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.title, sum(ec_max_contribution) from funding_schemes as t1 join projects as t2 on t1.code = t2.ec_fund_scheme group by t1.title ORDER BY sum(ec_max_contribution) DESC LIMIT 1
generated_sql:
SELECT f.title, SUM(p.ec_contribution) AS total_funding
FROM projects p
JOIN project_programmes pp ON p.unics_id = pp.project
JOIN programmes pr ON pp.programme = pr.code
JOIN funding_schemes f ON pr.code = f.code
GROUP BY f.title
ORDER BY total_funding DESC
LIMIT 1;
exec_status: SQL Error: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column p.ec_contribution does not exist

question: Show the amount of funding and corresponding list of industrial partners that have worked on projects in order from the most funding to the least funding
ground_truth: SELECT t1.member_name, p.ec_max_contribution FROM project_members as t1 join activity_types as t2 on t1.activity_type = t2.code join projects as p on t1.project = p.unics_id WHERE t2.code = 'PRC' ORDER BY ec_max_contribution DESC

question: Which non-EU entity has recieved the most funding through a project?

LIMIT 1;
